mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate for equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with rate increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0 percent, labor market slack triggers immediate risk-off positioning, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales signals robust aggregate demand, which sustains corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, a decline in the industrial production index reflects capacity constraints and slowing output, which precedes negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The negative signal reverses to positive returns during late-cycle phases, or the mechanism shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when filtered to late-cycle regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Higher consumer price index levels indicate persistent inflation, forcing markets to price in tighter financial conditions and reducing short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL shows zero or positive correlation with 5-day forward returns, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.3 million units, construction sector weakness signals a broader demand contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where nonfarm payrolls rise while the unemployment rate simultaneously increases indicates labor market deterioration and hidden slack, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive 60-day returns in > 50% of cases, or the interaction term adds no explanatory power over individual series in regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, federal funds rates above 5.0 percent suppress equity valuations through higher borrowing costs, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive or flat when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0 during tightening phases, or the regime-filtered signal fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.04.
